---
title: "local_vs_nonlocal"
author: "James McQuilkin"
date: "2026-03-24"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(h3jsr)
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(here)

#Load NYC boundary, dissolve into one outline and set to WGS84
nynta <- read_sf(here("analysis/data/raw_data/nynta2020_25d/nynta2020.shp"))

nyc_outline <- nynta %>%
  st_make_valid() %>%
  st_union() %>%
  st_transform(crs = 4326)

#Load Twitter Data
files <- list.files(here("analysis/data/raw_data/project_data/"), pattern = "*.csv.gz", full.names = TRUE)
df <- bind_rows(lapply(files, read_csv))

#Gather unique home IDs, covert to point, and bundle into one table
unique_homes <- unique(df$home)
home_coords  <- h3jsr::cell_to_point(unique_homes)

home_lookup <- data.frame(home = unique_homes) %>%
  st_as_sf(geometry = home_coords, crs = 4326)

#Local or non-local
sf_use_s2(FALSE)#I had to do this because the next step wouldn't work with s2 on
inside_nyc <- st_within(home_lookup, nyc_outline, sparse = FALSE)

home_lookup$is_local <- as.vector(inside_nyc)
table(home_lookup$is_local)  # FALSE = non-local, TRUE = local

#Join the TRUE/FALSE result back to every row in the main dataset
home_lookup_results <- home_lookup %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(home, is_local)

df <- df %>%
  left_join(home_lookup_results, by = "home") %>%
  mutate(user_type = ifelse(is_local, "local", "non-local"))

table(df$user_type)

write_csv(df, here("analysis/data/derived_data/twitter_with_locality.csv"))
```
